finding: AppError/ApiError/ApiResult definitions + all impls (new/with_details/status_code/error_code/convenience ctors/database_error/internal_with_id/Display/Error/IntoResponse/From<sqlx::Error>/From<Box<dyn Error>>/From<AppError> for (StatusCode,AppError)/to_api_error) diffed line-for-line against the pre-sdk-extraction type.rs; identical modulo T-3 doc fence.

APP_DATA_DIR Lazy differs from baseline ONLY by the T-1 app-name substitution (join(".ziee") -> join(format!(".{}", app_name()))); poison-recovery, set/get bodies, SERVER_ADDR, accessors byte-identical.
Both ui + desktop types.ts regenerate byte-identical to the committed baseline under B1 AND under pristine HEAD; the client-consumed API contract is unchanged.

The redaction guarantee is preserved: database_error/internal_with_id/From<sqlx::Error>/From<Box<dyn Error>> still route through the trace_id path and never embed inner Display; the 4 leak-regression tests moved verbatim and PASS in ziee-core.
From<sqlx::Error> maps RowNotFound->not_found and everything else->database_error unchanged; sqlx is a types-only dep (no query! macros) so ziee-core stays build-DB-free.

ensure_app_name() (Once) is invoked by BOTH get_app_data_dir and set_app_data_dir shims, guaranteeing set_app_name("ziee") runs before the APP_DATA_DIR Lazy first initializes via any ziee path; the "app" fallback is unreachable from ziee.

sse_event_enum $crate path retargeted to $crate::macros::pascal_to_camel_case (T-2); $crate resolves to ziee_core at ziee call sites where the helper is public. Verified by green cargo check of all 11 call sites.

Moved #[macro_export] macros re-exported at BOTH lib.rs and main.rs crate roots (the ziee package compiles a lib AND a bin from the same tree); crate::sse_event_enum!/impl_string_to_enum!/impl_json_from! all resolve in both targets.

make_transparent!/impl_json_option_from!/define_extension_content! correctly RETAINED in ziee (they reference crate::common::types::JsonOption / chat MessageContentData); pub(crate) use make_transparent preserved.

AppError/ApiError carry no JsonSchema derive so they are absent from the OpenAPI spec; path-set and schema-set are equal between baseline and regen (order-insensitive JSON equality True) — no route/type/schema added, removed, or renamed by B1.

openapi.json JSON key ORDER differs from committed baseline. PRE-EXISTING: pristine HEAD (B1 reverted) also fails to reproduce the committed baseline (357-line reorder); two rebuilds of identical source are byte-stable, so ordering is a deterministic function of the linkme registration/dependency graph. Adding ziee-core perturbs it, but zero semantic change. Baseline needs re-capture (flagged to orchestrator).

ApiError omitted from the re-export (T-4) to satisfy unused_imports=deny; remaining imports (schemars::JsonSchema, serde::{Deserialize,Serialize}) are all still used by the retained PaginationQuery; cargo check clean (no unused-import errors).

7 error tests + test_app_data_dir moved to ziee-core and PASS (cargo test -p ziee-core: 7 unit + 1 app_state + 2 ignored doctests). docker/nginx/max_file_upload tests stay in ziee (read ziee-relative docker files); ziee lib test target compiles green.
set_app_name/app_name introduce no 'ziee-chat' or hardcoded-name leak into the SDK; the app name is injected by the consumer, satisfying the app-agnostic requirement; ziee passes "ziee".

ziee-core deps pin ziee's catalog versions (axum 0.8.4, serde 1.0.219, serde_json 1.0.141, uuid 1.18, sqlx 0.8.6 default-features=false, once_cell 1.21.3, dirs 6.0.0, tracing 0.1); `cd sdk && cargo check --workspace` exits 0 and skeleton-server still builds framework-only.
